_source: Curated from a saved LinkedIn webarchive (2026-06). NOT live-fetched — this file is the contract (content tokens / slugs for copy). Edit here; the site renders from these slugs.
name: Robert DeLanghe
headline: I ship systems where agents write the code — I build the tooling to trust them in production.
intro: I'm Bobby. I became a software engineer by chasing the parts of code that feel like math — type systems, proofs, recursion, the self-referential knots where a thing contains itself and still holds together. I go down those paths because they're beautiful, not because anyone asked; the useful part shows up on its own. Lately it showed up as agents.
summary: At Aura, I write production commerce code with AI assistance, backed by a contract-and-validation layer I built that enforces correctness in CI. This work is available in open-source form as prx and the bounded-systems libraries.

experience.what: Capability security for AI agents — software delivered by agents, governed like infrastructure. The same discipline I ship at Aura, generalised into open tooling.
bullets: Designed and built a capability-security toolchain — prx, guest-room, claude-box — that bounds what an AI agent can do by the scoped door references it holds: authority is the reference, not a string-matched rule, so an agent can only do what its references allow — nothing it can reach by guessing a name.
Bound privileged actions to a signed owner and an auditable in-toto/SLSA provenance chain (ocap-provenance) — who authorized what is enforced in CI, not by convention.

experience.what: On Commerce — the team behind Aura's website, commerce-routing, and business-operations systems. I ship the integration and automation backbone, and built the contract-and-validation layer behind inventory-purchasing.
bullets: Ship the commerce and supply-chain backbone — NetSuite, Amazon, and Shopify integrations; inbound, Tactical, and LTL automation; EDI; and fulfillment daemons.

Replaced Aura's spreadsheet-driven supply plan (the system behind inventory-purchasing) with one source of truth that compiles into every downstream artifact — schemas, docs, tests, agent context — with CI blocking anything that drifts out of sync.
Built the auditing in: generated outputs are checked for provenance and against their contract, and sign-off is bound to an owner role enforced in CI rather than to a person.
The changes themselves are written by AI agents, moving through that same pipeline to a reviewed PR — their output held to the same provenance and contract checks as everything else.

experience.what: Built the quality gates and developer experience for a 20-engineer team, spanning static-analysis CI, a design system, and frontend performance.
bullets: Built static-analysis gating into the PR pipeline, blocking defect classes before merge; critical bugs fell 20% and high-priority issues 25% across the 20-engineer team.

Shipped the frontend platform: a design system that cut task lead time 30%, a dashboard framework that took widget builds from a week to a day, and pagination and performance work that brought First Contentful Paint under a second.

experience.what: Built the events + revenue data layer for the arts foundation.
bullets: A unified CRM + Square-API ETL — cut payment-tracking time 80%, with P&L across 200+ events.

experience.what: Built just-in-time production automation for the fabrication studio.
bullets: Just-in-time production workflows — eliminated edition-numbering errors, from 10/month to zero.

experience.what: Built the publishing pipeline for the manufacturing publication.
bullets: A unified content pipeline — cut curation time 70% across 1000+ articles.

experience.what: Type theory in Haskell — using the type system to make invalid states unrepresentable. The intellectual root of the capability work: push correctness into the boundary so violations can't compile.
bullets: Type-driven design in Haskell — algebraic data types and typeclasses — the same instinct now applied to security seams: invalid states unrepresentable, sign-off un-forgeable.

education.what: Computational & parametric design — algorithmic design for digital fabrication.

skills: TypeScript · Python · Node · Postgres · Docker · AWS

seeking.label: Open to new roles
seeking.focus: Founding / senior engineering on agent infrastructure — agent safety and code provenance
seeking.detail: Senior engineering on teams building agentic products. I bring direct experience shipping agent-authored systems and the guardrails that make them safe to run in production. Brooklyn · NYC · remote.
